cards.title: Creativity Thrives With Kids, Not Despite Them
cards.body: A parent discovers that having children doesn't diminish creative thinking—it expands it through unexpected perspectives. Moments like a four-year-old comparing kale stalks to giraffes reveal how kids transform everyday observations into wonder.

cards.title: What Organizational Psychology Teaches About Parenting
cards.body: Industrial and organizational psychology offers surprising insights applicable to family dynamics. Your family functions like an organization, so management principles can improve parenting effectiveness.

cards.title: When Two Hours of TV After School Is Good Parenting
cards.body: Parents who understand their child's needs shouldn't feel pressured by popular parenting trends that contradict what works for their family. Meeting individual child needs matters more than following conventional wisdom.

cards.title: Summer Freedom Can Trigger Anxiety in Kids
cards.body: The lack of school-year structure during summer can quietly trigger anxiety in children and teens rather than providing relaxation. Understanding this helps parents support their kids through seasonal transitions.

cards.title: You May Be Blocking Your Adult Child's Growth
cards.body: Protecting adult children from struggles may actually prevent them from developing resilience and survival skills. Parents must balance support with allowing kids to face challenges independently.

cards.title: Postpartum Body Acceptance Over Bounce-Back Pressure
cards.body: A body-neutral approach helps new mothers prioritize self-care without pressure to return to pre-pregnancy figures. This perspective supports maternal wellbeing and realistic recovery expectations.

cards.title: Family Patterns Are Not Genetic Destiny
cards.body: Epigenetics and inherited family patterns don't determine your future—choice does. Breaking cycles requires understanding that repetition is optional, not inevitable.

cards.title: Children's Laureate Patrice Lawrence Addresses Teen Masculinity
cards.body: Author Patrice Lawrence, known for Orangeboy, brings expertise to the crisis of teenage masculinity and declining youth reading rates. His appointment as children's laureate signals focus on engaging young readers.

cards.title: Including Young People in Conversations About Death
cards.body: Shielding youth from mortality discussions postpones crucial life lessons until adulthood. Including children in end-of-life conversations builds emotional resilience and understanding of universal human experiences.

cards.title: Bluey Now Available in Australian Indigenous Language
cards.body: Five episodes of the award-winning children's cartoon have been released in an Australian Indigenous language for the first time. This increases representation and cultural accessibility for young viewers.

cards.title: Midnight Social Media Curfew Proposed for UK Teens
cards.body: The government proposes a midnight social media curfew for teens aged 16-17, with opt-out options. Campaigners debate whether this measure effectively protects teens or represents a missed opportunity for stronger protections.

cards.title: Reproductive Psychiatry Emerging as Maternal Mental Health Specialty
cards.body: Clinicians and advocates work to establish reproductive psychiatry as a formal subspecialty for maternal mental health. Evidence-based guidance ensures women receive proper support during pregnancy and postpartum periods.
